note: The grand main square is relatively flat with paved and cobbled sections, step-free to cross and enjoy, though the surrounding streets climb steeply; the altitude tires everyone. The square itself is manageable.
The cathedral is entered up steps from the plaza with limited ramp access and stone floors inside; partly accessible with assistance at best. Grand but stepped.
The Inca-colonial temple has some flat courtyard areas but is entered up steps and set on a slope with uneven stone; partly accessible on the ground level with help.
A vibrant covered market that is flat inside but crowded, with tight aisles, thresholds and uneven floors; navigable slowly but a squeeze for wheels and strollers.

The great Inca fortress on the hill above Cusco is a vast site of steep grassy slopes and massive uneven stonework reached by a climb; not accessible for wheels, and the altitude compounds it. Views from the road only.
The artist quarter climbs steep, narrow cobbled and stepped lanes to its viewpoint; charming but very hard for wheels and strenuous at altitude. Not accessible.
The famous Inca stonework lines steep, narrow cobbled pedestrian streets; viewable while navigating the slopes but the surfaces and gradients are hard for wheels and strollers.
A colonial-mansion museum entered up steps with stairs between floors; limited accessibility, partly viewable on the ground level. Historic building constraints.

The wonder is reached by train and bus, and while there are limited assisted-access arrangements, the citadel is fundamentally a site of steep stone terraces and steps; largely not accessible, though the lower viewpoints can sometimes be reached with significant assistance. Plan carefully.

The valley's Inca ruins are dramatic hillside terraces reached by steep stone stairs; the towns have some flat plazas but the sites themselves are not accessible. Valley drives and town squares are the accessible parts.
Several central ground-floor cafes, chocolate museums and shops around the plaza have step-free or single-step access on the flatter blocks; accessible pockets amid the hills.
